mappings.rationaleAI: African history connects to East African civilizations (A), coastal and interior developments (B), and European activities (C).
Political institutions connect to military kingships in East Africa (C.1.a).
Indigenous traditions connect to East African civilizations (A) and spread of Islam (B.1.b).
Islam connects to the Shirazi dynasty and coastal Islam (B.1.b).
British activities connect to European exploration and missions (C.1.c).
Empire connects to Portuguese occupation (B.1.c) and European influence (C).
Colonial exploitation connects to European activities (C).
Archaeological methods connect to sources (A) and Stone Age origins (B.2.a).
Relevant to subsection B1a-b on medieval commercial contacts with Arabia and the development of Muslim coastal trading cities.
